subject: Re: JoJo's House
body:
is the broker philipino... if so let jojo negotiateif
not ask jojo to talk to the owner,, if not you talk to
the sellers agent

--- DKIESQ@aol.com wrote:

> Am exasperated with the Broker and am not sure I
> trust the Broker all that  
> much.
>  
> 1. Broker does not follow directions.
>  
> 2.  We offered $350k
>  
> 3.    Mysteriously, Broker came back and said two
> other  offers were 
> submitted before ours, do we want to increase our
> offer?
>  
> 4.    Naturally, 1st Rich, then I told the broker,
> find  out what the two 
> offers are and we will tell you what and if to
> counter and when  you counter 
> remind the seller's agent that we are all cash no 
> contingencies.
>  
> 5.    Broker then says JoJo and Lynn and I (meaning
> the  broker) think that 
> your initial offer was too low and we should have
> offered $2  K above asking 
> $392K to make sure to get the house.  Once again, I
> ask the  broker to find out 
> what the offers are for.  After all the house has
> been  on the market for 94 
> weeks (albeit at $419K initially and 390K for the
> past few  weeks), and we 
> don't want to negotiate against ourselves.  We ask
> the  broker to at least find 
> out what number the other side is willing to accept
> to  seal the deal with an 
> all cash, no contingency bid..
>  
> 6.    The Broker then calls back again and says
> "there  is almost a contract 
> from another offeror, what do we want to offer?  I
> get  on the phone again and 
> ask what is required to get the deal done.  The 
> Broker does not answer.  He 
> simply says that if you think another $10K is 
> enough it is not.  I ask did 
> you ask how much is enough?  The Broker  again tells
> me that he JoJo and Lynn 
> felt that we should have offered $392 to  begin
> with.  I ask him what the other 
> side said is needed.  He said he  did not get a
> number.  I then tell the 
> broker to say to the seller's agent,  what if I get
> my buyer to offer $380K is that 
> enough, and if no, I tell the  broker to say would
> $385K be enough; if no, 
> then say would $390K be enough, if  no then say
> would $392K be enough.  Very 
> specific because Rich (and I) did  not believe that
> the broker even asked for any 
> final number from the seller's  agent. 
>  
> 7.    Today we speak to the Broker again and ask
> what  happened.  The broker 
> says, "I believe there is now a contract, what do we
>  want to offer, so that 
> the seller has our offer in case the contract does 
> not work out?"  I ask what 
> did he find out from the broker.  He does  not
> answer, but says that this is a 
> hot property and again he says the he and  lynn and
> JoJo wanted to offer 
> $392K.  Exasperated, I ask the Broker what  did the
> other side say about an 
> acceptable number.  Broker then says, the  other
> side will not tell me a number.  
> Rich (and I) believe that for some  reason the
> Broker is simply not asking the 
> question.  
>  
> I ask the Broker if I can speak to the Seller's
> agent directly, and to give  
> me the number and I am waiting to hear back from the
> Broker.
>  
> I know it is ridiculous, but if I cannot speak to
> seller's agent or If I  
> cannot find out a price, do you want me to offer
> above asking price of  $390K?
>  
> Please advise.
>  
> Darren
>  
>

body_html:
is the broker philipino... if so let jojo negotiateif
not ask jojo to talk to the owner,, if not you talk to
the sellers agent

--- DKIESQ@aol.com wrote:

Am exasperated with the Broker and am not sure I
trust the Broker all that
much.

  1. Broker does not follow directions.

  2. We offered $350k

  3. Mysteriously, Broker came back and said two

other offers were
submitted before ours, do we want to increase our
offer?

  1. Naturally, 1st Rich, then I told the broker,

find out what the two
offers are and we will tell you what and if to
counter and when you counter
remind the seller's agent that we are all cash no
contingencies.

  1. Broker then says JoJo and Lynn and I (meaning

the broker) think that
your initial offer was too low and we should have
offered $2 K above asking
$392K to make sure to get the house. Once again, I
ask the broker to find out
what the offers are for. After all the house has
been on the market for 94
weeks (albeit at $419K initially and 390K for the
past few weeks), and we
don't want to negotiate against ourselves. We ask
the broker to at least find
out what number the other side is willing to accept
to seal the deal with an
all cash, no contingency bid..

  1. The Broker then calls back again and says

"there is almost a contract
from another offeror, what do we want to offer? I
get on the phone again and
ask what is required to get the deal done. The
Broker does not answer. He
simply says that if you think another $10K is
enough it is not. I ask did
you ask how much is enough? The Broker again tells
me that he JoJo and Lynn
felt that we should have offered $392 to begin
with. I ask him what the other
side said is needed. He said he did not get a
number. I then tell the
broker to say to the seller's agent, what if I get
my buyer to offer $380K is that
enough, and if no, I tell the broker to say would
$385K be enough; if no,
then say would $390K be enough, if no then say
would $392K be enough. Very
specific because Rich (and I) did not believe that
the broker even asked for any
final number from the seller's agent.

  1. Today we speak to the Broker again and ask

what happened. The broker
says, "I believe there is now a contract, what do we
want to offer, so that
the seller has our offer in case the contract does
not work out?" I ask what
did he find out from the broker. He does not
answer, but says that this is a
hot property and again he says the he and lynn and
JoJo wanted to offer
$392K. Exasperated, I ask the Broker what did the
other side say about an
acceptable number. Broker then says, the other
side will not tell me a number.
Rich (and I) believe that for some reason the
Broker is simply not asking the
question.

I ask the Broker if I can speak to the Seller's
agent directly, and to give
me the number and I am waiting to hear back from the
Broker.

I know it is ridiculous, but if I cannot speak to
seller's agent or If I
cannot find out a price, do you want me to offer
above asking price of $390K?

Please advise.

Darren
